entries.notes: 2026-05-03 market-order opposition coverage wave; fixed mechanism-based selection from pre-registered panel hypotheses, independent of observed verdicts. MMT disputes broad claims that larger public sectors, tax capacity, or non-accommodating anti-inflation regimes mechanically depress real outcomes.

2026-05-03 market-order opposition coverage wave; fixed mechanism-based selection from pre-registered panel hypotheses, independent of observed verdicts. Market socialism disputes the small-state implication and predicts market allocation can coexist with socialized capital and public investment.

2026-05-03 market-order opposition coverage wave; fixed mechanism-based selection from pre-registered panel hypotheses, independent of observed verdicts. Degrowth treats GDP, investment, and private-credit outcomes as welfare-ambiguous, so these are conditional stakes rather than directional wins.

Marxian theory disputes that market-order fiscal and monetary proxies generally deliver broad real-economy gains once class and accumulation dynamics are considered.

methodology_gate.not_selected_by: observed verdicts or school score effect
methodology_gate.score_caution: Degrowth links are mixed/low-confidence because the outcomes are welfare-ambiguous under degrowth theory.
methodology_gate.selection_rule: Fixed 10 fiscal/monetary market-order panel hypotheses crossed with 4 under-covered schools.
methodology_gate.verdict_policy: No verdicts changed; only reciprocal position links and covers_claims were added.

degrowth.rationale: Degrowth treats GDP, investment, and private-credit outcomes as welfare-ambiguous, so these are conditional stakes rather than directional wins.

market_socialist.rationale: Market socialism disputes the small-state implication and predicts market allocation can coexist with socialized capital and public investment.

marxian.rationale: Marxian theory disputes that market-order fiscal and monetary proxies generally deliver broad real-economy gains once class and accumulation dynamics are considered.

mmt.rationale: MMT disputes broad claims that larger public sectors, tax capacity, or non-accommodating anti-inflation regimes mechanically depress real outcomes.
